Browse Topics Search

Media, agencies wrangle over fees, access

John Sullivan NICAR staff November 1996
Data & DatabasesInvestigative Methods

Journalists often confront exorbitant fees and privacy issues when accessing electronic records. An AP request for the INS database prompted a fee of $300,000, reflecting a pattern of agency resistance.

Journalists often confront exorbitant fees and privacy issues when accessing electronic records. An AP request for the INS database prompted a fee of $300,000, reflecting a pattern of agency resistance. Agencies often claim that redaction equates to creating new records, contrary to federal law, complicating access even further. Problems with accessing state records augment these challenges, exemplified by a restrictive residency rule in Tennessee and legal battles in Indiana.

Journalists must frequently resort to legal channels to obtain public records, underscoring the persistent barriers to data access.
